stories.title: Taiwan presses Trump for clarity after his Beijing summit leaves arms support ambiguous
stories.summary: The immediate fallout from Donald Trump's Beijing meetings is being felt most sharply in Taipei, where officials are now publicly arguing for continuity in US arms support. The broader signal from Washington and Beijing is that tensions may be lower than they were last month, but the summit did not resolve the core disputes over Taiwan, technology and strategic trust.

links.headline: Taiwan's Lai says he'd tell Trump he hopes to continue arms purchases from US, if given a chance
links.excerpt: AP captures Taipei's unusually direct public push to keep US arms sales on a steady course after Trump's comments.
links.imageAlt: Taiwan's Lai says he'd tell Trump he hopes to continue arms purchases from US, if given a chance

links.headline: No major breakthroughs in Trump-Xi summit, UN chief says
links.excerpt: This Reuters report frames the summit as a stabilizing exercise that lowered heat without delivering a strategic breakthrough.

links.headline: Trump’s shifting remarks on Taiwan are perfect for China to exploit
links.excerpt: The Guardian focuses on how Beijing can use mixed White House messaging to pressure Taiwan politically and psychologically.
links.imageAlt: Trump’s shifting remarks on Taiwan are perfect for China to exploit

stories.imageAlt: Taiwan's Lai says he'd tell Trump he hopes to continue arms purchases from US, if given a chance

stories.title: Brussels ratifies a tariff deal with Washington to head off a fresh Trump trade clash
stories.summary: European institutions have moved to lock in a tariff arrangement with the United States before Trump's next threatened escalation window. The deal may steady transatlantic trade in the short term, but it also shows how much leverage deadline politics still holds over Europe's economic planning.

links.headline: EU overcomes fierce internal debate to agree on tariff deal with the US
links.excerpt: AP lays out the political trade-off Europe made to cap tariffs and avoid a more disruptive confrontation with Washington.
links.imageAlt: EU overcomes fierce internal debate to agree on tariff deal with the US

links.headline: EU paves way to finalise US trade deal and avoid Trump tariff hike
links.excerpt: Reuters adds the legislative mechanics and the remaining vulnerability if Washington changes course again later this year.

links.headline: EU agrees to implement US trade deal struck last summer
links.excerpt: The Guardian emphasizes the internal European resistance that had to be overcome before the package could move.
links.imageAlt: EU agrees to implement US trade deal struck last summer

stories.imageAlt: EU overcomes fierce internal debate to agree on tariff deal with the US

stories.title: WHO warns the Congo-Uganda Ebola outbreak is bigger and older than first thought
stories.summary: Health officials now believe the Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo likely started weeks earlier than first recognized and is still expanding. That makes the problem less a contained flare-up than a fast-moving regional emergency with weak local health capacity, cross-border movement and no approved strain-specific treatment to lean on.

links.headline: WHO says risk of global spread of Ebola outbreak is low, but high at national, regional levels
links.excerpt: AP highlights the WHO's split risk assessment: lower global danger, but sharp concern for Congo, Uganda and nearby states.

links.headline: Ebola fears surge on the ground in Congo over rapid spread of a rare type
links.excerpt: This AP field report adds the local view from health workers dealing with scarce protection, distance and mistrust.
links.imageAlt: Ebola fears surge on the ground in Congo over rapid spread of a rare type

links.headline: Ebola likely circulating in DRC for two months, outbreak to grow, WHO says
links.excerpt: Reuters adds the most concrete timeline warning that the outbreak was probably spreading before authorities realized its scale.
links.imageAlt: Ebola likely circulating in DRC for two months, outbreak to grow, WHO says

links.headline: WHO says 600 suspected cases, 139 deaths in growing Ebola outbreak
links.excerpt: Al Jazeera summarizes the latest case counts and the implications of the WHO emergency posture.
links.imageAlt: WHO says 600 suspected cases, 139 deaths in growing Ebola outbreak

stories.imageAlt: Ebola fears surge on the ground in Congo over rapid spread of a rare type

stories.title: A drone alert over Vilnius shows how Ukraine spillover is unnerving NATO's eastern edge
stories.summary: A shelter alert in Lithuania's capital turned a familiar border-security concern into a stark public warning inside an EU and NATO capital. The incident reinforces that the war's drone geography now reaches far enough to create sudden political and aviation disruption even without a deliberate strike on alliance territory.

links.headline: Residents of Lithuania's capital told to shelter as drone alarm underlines NATO's eastern jitters
links.excerpt: AP details the shelter warning, airspace disruption and the official reaction inside Vilnius.
links.imageAlt: Residents of Lithuania's capital told to shelter as drone alarm underlines NATO's eastern jitters

links.headline: Ukrainian drone shot down by NATO jet over Estonia; Kyiv blames Moscow for steering it there
links.excerpt: Reuters provides the immediate regional context by tying Lithuania's alarm to the Estonian shootdown a day earlier.

links.headline: Lithuanian lawmakers shelter, Vilnius air traffic suspended due to drone incursion
links.excerpt: Defense News concentrates on the operational and air-defense implications for Baltic security planners.
links.imageAlt: Lithuanian lawmakers shelter, Vilnius air traffic suspended due to drone incursion

stories.imageAlt: Residents of Lithuania's capital told to shelter as drone alarm underlines NATO's eastern jitters

stories.title: NATO tries to steady allies as Washington pares back what it will commit in Europe
stories.summary: Allied officials are trying to cool alarm over US force changes in Europe, but the underlying message is still that Washington expects the continent to carry more of the load. The issue now is not only how many troops remain, but how much ready capability the US is still prepared to reserve for a European crisis.

links.headline: Poland welcomes U.S. statements that troop reduction there is temporary
links.excerpt: AP shows how quickly allied reassurance has become necessary in Warsaw after deployment plans were abruptly changed.
links.imageAlt: Poland welcomes U.S. statements that troop reduction there is temporary

links.headline: NATO's top officer doesn't expect more American drawdowns beyond the 5,000 troops Trump announced
links.excerpt: This AP report captures the alliance effort to define the current cut as limited rather than the start of a broader retreat.
links.imageAlt: NATO's top officer doesn't expect more American drawdowns beyond the 5,000 troops Trump announced

links.headline: US force adjustments in Europe will be gradual and structured, Rutte says
links.excerpt: Reuters adds Mark Rutte's attempt to reassure allies that the force changes will be sequenced rather than chaotic.

links.headline: Secretary General highlights how Allies are stepping up ahead of Foreign Ministers meeting in Sweden
links.excerpt: The NATO statement lays out the official alliance line on burden-sharing, spending and preparations for the next ministerial meeting.
links.imageAlt: Secretary General highlights how Allies are stepping up ahead of Foreign Ministers meeting in Sweden

stories.imageAlt: Poland welcomes U.S. statements that troop reduction there is temporary

stories.title: Xi and Putin use their Beijing summit to double down on energy ties and anti-US messaging
stories.summary: With Trump only just out of Beijing, Xi and Putin moved quickly to show that China-Russia coordination remains intact on energy, security and diplomatic framing. Even without a headline pipeline breakthrough, the meeting was designed to signal that neither capital intends to let US summitry pull them apart strategically.

links.headline: Putin and Xi hail their friendship and growing energy trade at their meeting in Beijing
links.excerpt: AP focuses on the public choreography and the emphasis both leaders placed on energy and strategic trust.
links.imageAlt: Putin and Xi hail their friendship and growing energy trade at their meeting in Beijing

links.headline: Xi and Putin unite in criticism of US on nuclear, security issues
links.excerpt: Reuters adds the sharper anti-US positioning and the reminder that major energy questions remain unresolved.
links.quality: Reuters report carried by Investing Canada

links.headline: Putin, Xi Hold Beijing Summit With Energy, Iran In Focus
links.excerpt: RFE/RL frames the visit as both an energy discussion and a broader geopolitical response to Trump's regional diplomacy.

stories.imageAlt: Putin and Xi hail their friendship and growing energy trade at their meeting in Beijing

stories.title: The FAA cuts traffic in major markets while rushing controller hiring and training fixes
stories.summary: US aviation officials are now treating air-traffic strain as an operational risk serious enough to justify broad traffic reductions. At the same time, the agency is trying to show that longer-term staffing and training fixes are finally moving from promises into a more structured workforce plan.

links.headline: FAA reducing air traffic by 10% across 40 'high-volume' markets during government shutdown
links.excerpt: This AP-syndicated report is the clearest account of the immediate capacity cut and the safety rationale behind it.

links.headline: FAA Releases Bold, New Air Traffic Controller Hiring Plan
links.excerpt: The FAA's own hiring plan sets out the agency's new controller targets and the argument for a broader workforce reset.
links.imageAlt: FAA Releases Bold, New Air Traffic Controller Hiring Plan

links.headline: New FAA Program Moves to Strengthen Controller Pipeline, Standardize Training
links.excerpt: This companion FAA release explains the training pipeline changes meant to support the staffing push over the next few years.
links.imageAlt: New FAA Program Moves to Strengthen Controller Pipeline, Standardize Training

stories.imageAlt: FAA Releases Bold, New Air Traffic Controller Hiring Plan

stories.title: ASML says AI, satellite and robotics demand will keep chip supply tight
stories.summary: The global chip-equipment bottleneck is not easing yet, with ASML now warning that demand across several AI-adjacent sectors is still outpacing what manufacturers can deliver. The implication is that the semiconductor squeeze is broadening beyond pure datacenter build-outs and could keep capacity pricing and export-control politics elevated.

links.headline: ASML CEO sees tight supply in booming chip market as AI demand soars
links.excerpt: Reuters provides the clearest topline from Christophe Fouquet that supply conditions will stay tense for the foreseeable future.

links.headline: Exclusive-ASML CEO sees tight supply in booming chip market as AI demand soars
links.excerpt: This carry adds detail on which end markets, including satellites and robotics, are reinforcing the supply squeeze.

links.headline: ASML reports €8.8 billion total net sales and €2.8 billion net income in Q1 2026
links.excerpt: ASML's latest official results provide the company-side demand and backlog context behind today's warning on constrained supply.

stories.title: Lambda's Hudson River Trading win shows how scarce Nvidia capacity is still being allocated
stories.summary: The newest AI-infrastructure deals are not about abstract model hype so much as who can actually secure access to the latest Blackwell systems at scale. Lambda's HRT agreement shows that scarce Nvidia-backed compute is still being steered toward customers with high-value, latency-sensitive workloads and deep budgets.

links.headline: Lambda wins cloud deal with Hudson River Trading to supply access to Nvidia chips
links.excerpt: Reuters reports the scale of the arrangement and what it says about current demand for top-tier AI hardware access.

links.headline: Lambda wins cloud deal with Hudson River Trading to supply access to Nvidia chips
links.excerpt: This Reuters carry adds context on Hudson River Trading's existing cloud footprint and why the deal matters strategically.
links.quality: Reuters report carried by local radio outlet
links.imageAlt: Lambda wins cloud deal with Hudson River Trading to supply access to Nvidia chips

links.headline: Lambda partners with Hudson River Trading to power quantitative research and development
links.excerpt: Lambda's own announcement shows how the company wants to pitch the partnership as an AI trading and research platform win.
links.imageAlt: Lambda partners with Hudson River Trading to power quantitative research and development

stories.imageAlt: Lambda wins cloud deal with Hudson River Trading to supply access to Nvidia chips

stories.title: SpaceX lines up Starship V3 for a pre-IPO test that matters for launch cadence and investor confidence
stories.summary: SpaceX's next Starship test is carrying unusual weight because it is both a technical milestone and a market story ahead of the company's planned IPO. The upgraded vehicle is being sold as a meaningful step toward higher launch cadence, more ambitious payload tests and a stronger case that Starship can move from spectacle to repeatable system.

links.headline: SpaceX’s upgraded Starship V3 ready for debut launch ahead of IPO
links.excerpt: Reuters frames the launch as a technology and capital-markets test rolled into one.

links.headline: SpaceX prepares for pre-IPO Starship launch
links.excerpt: Semafor focuses on the investor read-through and why this specific test matters ahead of the planned float.
links.imageAlt: SpaceX prepares for pre-IPO Starship launch

links.headline: What time is SpaceX's Starship V3 launch on May 21? (Starship Flight 12 timeline)
links.excerpt: Space.com adds the updated launch timing and mission specifics for the first flight of the V3 vehicle.
links.imageAlt: What time is SpaceX's Starship V3 launch on May 21? (Starship Flight 12 timeline)

stories.imageAlt: SpaceX prepares for pre-IPO Starship launch

stories.title: Australia leans on China for jet fuel as Canberra keeps shoring up near-term energy resilience
stories.summary: Canberra is still managing the downstream effects of the regional fuel shock with emergency-style supply arrangements rather than pretending the market has normalized. Securing extra jet fuel from China underlines how exposed Australian transport and logistics remain to wider energy disruptions, even as officials try to stabilize inventories and fertilizer inputs.

links.headline: China Agrees to Supply Australia Over 600,000 Barrels of Jet Fuel
links.excerpt: Rigzone gives the headline shipment volume and the basic supply-security significance for Australia's aviation system.
links.imageAlt: China Agrees to Supply Australia Over 600,000 Barrels of Jet Fuel

links.headline: Australia secures more jet fuel from China, urea from Brunei
links.excerpt: Reuters adds the fertilizer angle and shows the package as part of a broader effort to reduce near-term supply stress.
links.imageAlt: Australia secures more jet fuel from China, urea from Brunei

links.headline: PM Albanese secures 600,000 barrels of jet fuel from China as Australia shores up supplies
links.excerpt: This carry packages the same move as a prime-ministerial supply intervention rather than just a market-development story.
links.imageAlt: PM Albanese secures 600,000 barrels of jet fuel from China as Australia shores up supplies

links.headline: Weekly fuel price monitoring report - 8 May 2026
links.excerpt: The ACCC's monitoring report provides the regulator-side context for how fragile domestic fuel conditions had already become.

stories.imageAlt: China Agrees to Supply Australia Over 600,000 Barrels of Jet Fuel

stories.title: A NSW inquiry says childcare property and investor incentives are distorting safety and access
stories.summary: A New South Wales inquiry has pushed the childcare debate past staffing complaints and into the economics of who owns centres, where they get built and how profits are extracted. The report argues that investor logic is not just raising costs but steering services away from poorer areas while weakening safety and oversight outcomes.

links.headline: NSW childcare inquiry scrutinises role of property developers, investors in safety failures
links.excerpt: ABC reports the inquiry's core finding that growth and revenue incentives are cutting across quality and child safety.
links.imageAlt: NSW childcare inquiry scrutinises role of property developers, investors in safety failures

links.headline: Budget Estimates 2025-26 – Additional hearing questions for Hon Prue Car MP
links.excerpt: These parliamentary materials provide official regulator and enforcement context for the policy pressures behind today's inquiry report.

stories.imageAlt: NSW childcare inquiry scrutinises role of property developers, investors in safety failures

stories.title: An audit says NSW's flagship post-flood housing program has not delivered the homes promised
stories.summary: The political promise behind the Northern Rivers recovery package was that flood-hit communities would see safer housing delivered at scale. The audit suggests the state instead built a large program without the planning discipline needed to deliver on time, leaving the headline commitment badly exposed.

links.headline: NSW Auditor-General finds flaws in Australia's costliest disaster recovery program
links.excerpt: ABC lays out the blunt audit finding that not one promised home or lot had been delivered by the end of March.
links.imageAlt: NSW Auditor-General finds flaws in Australia's costliest disaster recovery program

links.headline: NSW Reconstruction Authority annual report 2024-25
links.excerpt: The authority's own reporting gives the program metrics and implementation frame against which the auditor's criticism lands.

stories.imageAlt: NSW Auditor-General finds flaws in Australia's costliest disaster recovery program

stories.title: BARMM declares calamity status as heat and fuel stress deepen the Philippines' power crunch
stories.summary: The Philippine energy strain is no longer just a warning about summer reserves but a broader governance and cost-of-living problem hitting whole regions differently. BARMM's calamity declaration and fresh grid alerts in the Visayas show how fuel costs, heat and plant outages are feeding into a more persistent national power emergency.

links.headline: BARMM under state of calamity due to oil crisis, extreme heat
links.excerpt: GMA covers the regional declaration and the way fuel prices and climate stress are being linked in official policy language.
links.imageAlt: BARMM under state of calamity due to oil crisis, extreme heat

links.headline: Visayas grid back on yellow alert
links.excerpt: Philstar adds the latest generation shortfall numbers and shows that reserve margins remain thin even outside the main island grid.
links.imageAlt: Visayas grid back on yellow alert

links.headline: DOE: More rotating power outages expected
links.excerpt: This earlier Philstar report provides the national warning that the disruptions are expected to keep recurring in coming months.
links.imageAlt: DOE: More rotating power outages expected

stories.imageAlt: BARMM under state of calamity due to oil crisis, extreme heat

stories.title: Investigators push charges over Senate gunfire as the Duterte impeachment crisis keeps widening
stories.summary: The Philippine institutional crisis is no longer confined to the vice-president's trial itself; the armed chaos around the Senate is now becoming its own legal and political front. That matters because it deepens public doubts about whether the state's security and constitutional machinery can manage elite conflict without further escalation.

links.headline: PNP pushes charges vs. OSAA staff over Senate shooting
links.excerpt: GMA provides the newest law-enforcement turn, with investigators now targeting staff linked to the gunfire inside the Senate complex.
links.imageAlt: PNP pushes charges vs. OSAA staff over Senate shooting

links.headline: Philippine Senate convenes as impeachment court to try vice president as political storm rages
links.excerpt: AP captures the broader constitutional stakes and why the case is now unfolding inside a highly unstable political environment.
links.imageAlt: Philippine Senate convenes as impeachment court to try vice president as political storm rages

links.headline: Senators don robes as Sara Duterte impeachment trial opens
links.excerpt: Philstar adds the chamber-level procedural detail and domestic political texture around the start of the trial.
links.imageAlt: Senators don robes as Sara Duterte impeachment trial opens

stories.imageAlt: PNP pushes charges vs. OSAA staff over Senate shooting
